Task 6: Create flat-color graphics pipeline

Add VkPipeline and VkPipelineLayout for flat-color rendering to VulkanRenderer:
- 144-byte push constants (projection mat4 + model mat4 + color vec4)
- Descriptor set layout for lighting UBO (set 0, binding 0)
- vec2 vertex input, triangle list topology, dynamic viewport/scissor
- No culling, src-alpha blending, dynamic rendering with swap chain format
- Loads flat.vert.spv and flat.frag.spv compiled SPIR-V shaders

Add helper methods:
- readSPVFile(): reads binary SPIR-V files from disk
- createShaderModule(): wraps SPIR-V code in a VkShaderModule

Pipeline and layout are properly destroyed in cleanupVulkan().

Task 5: Port shaders to Vulkan GLSL and build SPIR-V

Extract all 5 inline GLSL 330 shader programs from GLRenderer.cpp into
standalone GLSL 450 files under assets/shaders/:

- flat.vert/frag: flat-color with per-pixel lighting via push constants
- textured.vert/frag: sprite rendering with normal map support
- text.vert/frag: glyph atlas text rendering
- vertcolor.vert/frag: per-vertex-color geometry
- blit.vert/frag: fullscreen passthrough blit
- lighting.glsl: shared 32-light system (point + spot) included by
  flat.frag and textured.frag

Vulkan binding conventions:
- layout(push_constant) for per-draw data (projection, model, color)
- layout(std140, set=0, binding=0) for lighting UBO
- layout(set=1, binding=N) for texture samplers

Updated compile_shaders() CMake function to support INCLUDE_DIRS (-I flags)
and DEPENDS (header dependencies), and target Vulkan 1.3. Added post-build
step to copy compiled .spv files to output directory.

commit.message:
Task 4: Command infrastructure and frame synchronization

Add Vulkan command pool, double-buffered command buffers (2 frames in
flight), and per-frame synchronization primitives (imageAvailable and
renderFinished semaphores, inFlight fence).

Implement the full frame loop in display():
- Wait for in-flight fence, acquire swap chain image
- Record command buffer with dynamic rendering (clear to stored color)
- Image layout transitions via VkImageMemoryBarrier2
- Submit with semaphore synchronization, present to swap chain

Enable VkPhysicalDeviceDynamicRenderingFeatures and
VkPhysicalDeviceSynchronization2Features during device selection.

Handle swap chain recreation on window resize via GLFW framebuffer
size callback.

The Vulkan backend now clears to a solid color each frame when active.
Draw-call methods remain no-ops for future tasks.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix FOV corner-clipping: block rays that squeeze through diagonal wall gaps

Bresenham's line makes diagonal steps (both x and y advance in one
iteration). When this happens, the ray was not checking the two
cardinal-neighbour tiles it passes between, allowing it to "see through"
touching room corners.

Fix: before each diagonal step, check both cardinal neighbours. If both
are walls (or out-of-bounds), return false — the ray is blocked.

Also extracted isWall() helper; out-of-bounds tiles are treated as opaque.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(keepalive): hold strong reference to ping task to prevent GC

asyncio.create_task returns a Task that the event loop holds only via a
weak reference. Without a strong reference the GC can silently discard
the task before it ever pings, making the keepalive a no-op.

Store the task in a module-level set and discard it via done_callback so
the reference is released cleanly when (if) the loop ever exits.

  saturates the thread pool via internal par_chunks(512)

* docs(benchmarks): replace interpolation comparison with power-of-two fair test

Replace the SRHT vs exact comparison table in README with results from
the new methodology: run both modes at d in {128,256,512,1024} where
next_power_of_two(d)=d, so both modes produce exactly n=d codes with
identical bit budgets and codebooks.

Key findings:
- d<=256: differences within noise (+-0.012 R@1), no consistent winner
- d=512: exact consistently +0.2-0.9 pp R@1
- d=1024 b=4: exact +3.8 pp R@1 — meaningful advantage for Haar-random QR
- d=1024 b=8: both modes at 79.6% R@1, indistinguishable near ceiling
- Speed: SRHT 1.1-17.9x faster; gap widens with d (O(d log d) vs O(d^2))

Also rewrites compare_quantizers_fair.py to use this methodology with
enforce-via-assert_power_of_two(), direct delta table (no interpolation),
and 100k synthetic Gaussian corpus at all dims.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(engine): v0.5 sprint — #29 #30 #31 #33 #34 + fast_mode dequantize fix

### #33 WAL write coalescing
Pre-build entire batch into one Vec<u8>, then one write_all + one flush
per append_batch call. Eliminates per-entry syscall overhead.

### #29 SIMD unpack_mse_indices
AVX2 variant for b=4: 16 packed bytes → 32 u16 per iteration via
_mm_unpacklo/hi_epi8 + _mm256_cvtepu8_epi16. Scalar fallback unchanged.

### #34 Cross-query parallelism in search_batch
For ANN or small-N (≤SEQ_THRESHOLD=20k) paths, par_iter() across queries.
Large-N brute-force stays serial (inner par_chunks saturates Rayon there).

### #31 Range index for metadata
BTreeMap<u64, Vec<u32>> per numeric field using IEEE 754 ordered-key
encoding (f64_to_ord). extract_range_condition() routes $gt/$gte/$lt/$lte
filters through get_range_candidates() in resolve_filter_via_index().

### #30 Auto query planner
_use_ann: Option<bool> (None=auto, True=force ANN, False=force brute).
auto_use_ann() returns true when index exists + N≥10k + delta≤20% of N.
Module-level constants: AUTO_ANN_THRESHOLD=10_000, AUTO_ANN_MAX_DELTA=20%.

### fix(quantizer): fast_mode dequantize
dequantize() short-circuits to MSE-only when fast_mode=True, preventing
a panic on the zero-length QJL slice during rerank.

prevents deadlock and manages core sharing automatically.

* fix(python): change defaults to rerank=False, fast_mode=True (minimum disk out-of-box)

Previous defaults (rerank=True, fast_mode=False) stored INT8 raw vectors and QJL
residuals for every new database, making the zero-config experience heavier than
needed. New defaults match the paper Figure 5 allocation and minimize disk usage:

  rerank=False  — no raw vector storage; pure compressed codes
  fast_mode=True — MSE-only quantization; fastest ingest, minimum disk
  quantizer_type=None ("dense") — unchanged

Users who need higher recall explicitly opt in:
  rerank=True           → +5–25 pp R@1, ~2–4× more disk
  fast_mode=False       → +5–10 pp R@1 at d≥1536 when rerank=False

Update PYTHON_API.md and CONFIGURATION.md to reflect new defaults throughout:
parameter table, fast_mode×rerank combination table, all preset examples,
and the decision flowchart.

fix: always append signal protocol instructions to every prompt

Signal instructions (status.json updates, inbox checks, headless mode)
were only in the generic fallback. Custom prompts via --prompt,

so headless teams never updated status.json.

Now signalInstructions() is appended to ALL resolved prompts regardless
of source. The 4-tier prompt resolution still works the same way for
the mission/task content — signal protocol is always added on top.

commit.message:
fix: have all bots subscribe to all relays and follow all users (#75)

## Problem

`subscribeToRelays` only used the **first bot** to subscribe to relays.
Because the `relays` table previously had a unique constraint on `url`
alone, all subsequent bots were skipped once a relay URL was already
recorded — meaning only one of the 40+ bots would ever join a relay.
`followAccounts` was already correct (each bot follows each user
individually), so only the relay subscription logic needed fixing.

## Changes

### `src/lib/schema.ts`
- Added `botUsername` column to the `relays` table.
- Changed the unique constraint from `url` alone to `(botUsername,
url)`, enabling per-bot relay tracking.

### `src/lib/db.ts`
- Updated `RelayRow` type to include `botUsername`.
- `upsertRelay` now accepts a `botUsername` parameter.
- `getAllRelays` now accepts an optional `botUsername` filter.
- `getAcceptedRelays` deduplicates results by `inboxUrl` to avoid
delivering to the same relay inbox multiple times now that each relay
has one row per bot.
- `removeRelay` is now scoped per-bot.
- Added `getRelayStatusSummary` and `getFollowingStatusSummary` helpers
used by the new status page.

### `src/lib/federation.ts`
- `subscribeToRelays` now resolves each relay actor once (using the
first bot as a signing identity) and sends a Follow from **every** bot.
- The already-subscribed check uses `"botUsername:relayUrl"` composite
keys so new bots always get properly subscribed even if the relay URL
already exists in the table.

### `src/app/status/page.tsx` (new)
- Adds a `/status` page showing three sections:
- **Relay Subscriptions** — per-relay accepted/pending/rejected counts
with a `bots / total` ratio (highlighted amber when not all bots have
subscribed).
  - **Account Follows** — same breakdown for each configured handle.
- **RSS Feeds** — table of all bots with their display name and feed
URL.

### `src/app/layout.tsx`
- Adds a **Status** link to the nav bar next to the existing **Stats**
link.

### `drizzle/0008_relay_per_bot.sql` (new migration)
- Truncates the `relays` table (data will be re-established on next
startup).
- Drops the old `relays_url_unique` constraint.
- Adds the `bot_username` column (`NOT NULL`).
- Adds the new `relays_bot_username_url_unique` constraint on
`(bot_username, url)`.

### `drizzle/meta/` (generated)
- `0008_snapshot.json` and updated `_journal.json` generated by
`drizzle-kit generate --name=relay_per_bot`.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: icco <20201+icco@users.noreply.github.com>
